faq_sub: Common questions from warehouse, logistics, and manufacturing teams considering automation.
faq_q1: How much does automation typically cost?
faq_a1: Costs vary widely — from a few thousand euros for software-based process automation to hundreds of thousands for physical robotics. That is exactly why an Automation Opportunity Assessment comes first: to understand what is realistic and worth pursuing before any major spending.
faq_q2: Do we need to have robots or vendors already selected?
faq_a2: No. We can work with you from scratch, or alongside your existing vendor conversations. The assessment helps you evaluate options with clear criteria rather than vendor marketing.
faq_q3: Can you work with our existing systems and vendors?
faq_a3: Yes. We specialize in integration architecture — connecting new automation layers with existing WMS, ERP, and operational tools your team already uses.
faq_q4: What if automation turns out not to be viable?
faq_a4: We will tell you honestly. If the numbers do not support automation at this stage, you receive a clear explanation of why and what to revisit in 12–18 months. That clarity is worth the assessment cost on its own.
faq_q5: What industries do you focus on?
faq_a5: Primarily warehousing, fulfillment, logistics, and light manufacturing — industries where process efficiency and throughput directly affect profitability.
